Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and metabolic acidosis (low CO₂, high chloride). Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, and abnormal urine appearance, indicating possible nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy, though HbA1c is low. No evidence of urinary infection. Findings warrant further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying metabolic or renal pathology.
